danger_signals.visual_form: Graveyard imagery, ghosts, demons, evil iconography
danger_signals.meaning: High-risk play zone; thematic danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Red coloration under top bumper in Nightmare table
danger_signals.meaning: Visceral danger indicator; censored to blue in SNES port

safety_signals.visual_form: Rocket launch, planets, space exploration imagery
safety_signals.meaning: Thematic safety; forward progress, exploration as positive state

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ghosts, demons, graves, crosses (removed in SNES)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each of four pinball tables presents distinct thematic world with unique aesthetic (space, industrial, music, supernatural)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ball physics and table layout remain consistent across themes; world-state expressed purely through visual theming and environmental storytelling

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: SNES port removes red 'blood' imagery (changed to blue) and removes religious crosses from Nightmare table
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Table functionality unchanged; visual tone moderated for regional market standards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four distinct visual environments represent different world-states player can inhabit via table selection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each table offers unique challenge layout and visual feedback; no world-state hierarchy implied

environmental_storytelling.example: Ignition table communicates space exploration world through rocket, planet, and orbital imagery on play field; Steel Wheel uses train iconography and western motifs to establish industrial frontier setting
Nightmare table establishes danger/evil world-state through graveyard aesthetic, ghosts, demons, and originally visceral blood imagery (censored in SNES)
Menu displays 'Ignition', 'Steel Wheel', 'Beat Box', 'Nightmare' as world-state labels; 'Graveyard' subtitle on Nightmare table reinforces setting (naming inconsistency shows port variation)
Realistic ball physics applied uniformly across all tables ensures mechanical world-state remains stable regardless of visual theming

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves according to reasonably realistic physics; responds to bumpers and ramps with consistent mechanical feedback
Ghost and demon obstacles present thematic challenges; visual evil-alignment signals danger zones
Consistent mechanical response across all tables; no behavioral variation by theme

notes: Pinball Dreams communicates world-state primarily through visual theming rather than mechanical or behavioral variation. Four distinct thematic tables (Ignition/space, Steel Wheel/western-industrial, Beat Box/music, Nightmare/supernatural) represent different diegetic worlds player enters via selection. Regional censorship in SNES port (red→blue blood, cross removal) demonstrates how visual communication of danger/theme is culturally moderated. No faction conflicts or political situations present; world-states are thematically separated play environments rather than a unified contested world. Game uses visual storytelling (environmental aesthetic, iconography) exclusively; behavioral manifestation of world-state is minimal.
